_notes: AUM and capex figures sourced from public disclosures, annual reports, IPE Real Assets, Bloomberg and market data. Last enriched with live research March 2026. All figures approximate.

Macquarie GIG.aumNote: Ranked #1 infrastructure manager globally by IPE Real Assets Top 100 (2025, as at 31 Mar 2025)
Macquarie GIG.notes: Most active infrastructure investor in Australian RE. Developer and investor across wind, solar, storage. Launched Aula Energy (4 GW AU/NZ pipeline). GIG platform manages Potentia Energy JV.

Brookfield.notes: Completed Neoen acquisition April 4, 2025 (squeeze-out/delisting). Initial 53.12% stake closed Dec 30, 2024 (EUR 3.25B with Temasek). Neoen targeting 10 GW globally. Largest single RE deal in Australia.

BlackRock / GIP.notes: Global fund giant. Acquired GIP 2024. Growing Australian RE exposure. Controls Akaysha Energy (1 GW+ BESS pipeline - key CIS tender winner).

IFM Investors.notes: Australian industry super fund backed (17 funds). Significant infrastructure allocation. Domestic advantage - no FIRB issues.

AustralianSuper.notes: Largest Australian super fund. 10%+ infrastructure target. Increasing direct RE investment.

QIC.australiaCapex2024: A$12B RE portfolio; A$15B+ 5-year investment plan
QIC.notes: Queensland government-owned. Active RE infrastructure investor via PowAR consortium. QIC Infrastructure managing Punchs Creek solar+BESS JV (480MWp + 1.6GWh).

CBUS Super.notes: Construction sector super fund. Growing infrastructure RE allocation via IFM and direct mandates.

Aware Super.notes: Merged super fund. Expanding direct infrastructure mandate. Member of consortia for offshore wind and large-scale solar.

Future Fund.notes: Australian sovereign wealth fund. Member of PowAR consortium (with AGL and QIC). Passive long-duration investor.

Quinbrook.notes: Australia-focused RE developer/investor. Raised A$400M (Aug 2025). 1.5 GW+ operational/construction portfolio, 5 GW development pipeline. $3B deployment target.

Octopus Energy.notes: Received USD 650M commitment from Dutch pension APG (July 2025). Rapidly expanding Australian platform across wind, solar, BESS.

APG.notes: Dutch pension giant. Committed USD 650M to Octopus Australia Jul 2025. One of the largest pension funds globally.

Sembcorp.notes: Singapore utility. Agreed to acquire Alinta Energy Dec 2025 (A$6.5B EV / A$5.6B purchase price). Awaiting FIRB + ACCC approval, expected H1 2026. Alinta: 3.4 GW owned/contracted + 10.4 GW pipeline. Target 25 GW global renewables by 2028.

Amp Energy.notes: Global infrastructure investor active in Australian RE development.

Tepco / T-REX.notes: Japanese utility with major Australian RE pipeline acquisitions (5 GW+).

AGL Energy.notes: ASX-listed. Transitioning from coal. A$20B capex plan for RE transition. Part of PowAR consortium. CIS contract holder.

Origin Energy.notes: ASX-listed. Significant RE development pipeline. Building large BESS at Eraring site. Significant CIS contracts.

ENGIE.notes: French utility with long-standing Australian operations. Closing coal, investing in RE.

EDP Renewables.notes: Portuguese RE developer with Australian wind/solar portfolio.

Acciona.notes: Spanish developer with large Australian wind assets. MacIntyre Wind Farm (923 MW, QLD) - one of world's largest onshore wind farms. Bellwether 3 GW WA wind project proposed. Limited M&A appetite - primarily develops own projects.

Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners.notes: Danish infrastructure fund. Active in Australian offshore wind and large-scale RE.

CDPQ.notes: Quebec pension fund. Growing Australian RE infrastructure allocation.

Ares Management.notes: US alternative asset manager expanding infrastructure globally including Australia.

KKR.notes: US PE giant. Australian infrastructure team active. Increasing RE allocation.

Global summary.dominantInvestorTypes: Infrastructure funds (Macquarie, Brookfield, BlackRock), pension funds (APG, CDPQ, AustralianSuper), Asian utilities (Sembcorp, Tepco)
Global summary.newRegulatory2026: ACCC mandatory merger clearance regime (Jan 2026) adds approval requirement for major deals
Global summary.outlook: Strong demand expected to continue. 40 GW CIS target needs A$73B+ investment. AEMO forecasts 58 GW grid-scale wind+solar by 2030.
Global summary.cisRisk: ~28% FID rate on CIS-awarded projects to date - zombie project risk for lowest-cost/undercapitalised developers
